visual_checks: Check the PDF security settings for content copying and text extraction permissions
Look at the document restriction summary to see if accessibility permissions are enabled
Verify that the 'Content Copying for Accessibility' or equivalent permission is allowed
Check if any security encryption or password protection blocks assistive technology access
Review the full permissions panel to identify any restrictions that could impact screen reader access
pass_conditions: PDF security settings allow content extraction for accessibility purposes
The 'Content Copying for Accessibility' permission (or equivalent) is enabled
Assistive technologies such as screen readers can access the full text content of the document
If the PDF is password-protected, the accessibility permission is still explicitly enabled
No security settings prevent text-to-speech or braille output from accessing document content
fail_conditions: Content copying is disabled and the accessibility permission is also not set, blocking assistive technology
Text extraction is blocked by the PDF security settings
The accessibility permission flag is not set, preventing screen readers from accessing content
Security encryption prevents assistive technology from reading the document structure and text
The document requires a password to open and no accessible alternative is provided
na_conditions: The document is not a PDF
The PDF has no security restrictions applied
ne_conditions: The PDF security settings could not be read or extracted for evaluation
The document's permission flags could not be determined from the available data
common_mistakes: Applying content protection to prevent copying without enabling the separate accessibility permission
Using a PDF security tool that disables all content extraction including for assistive technology
Assuming that DRM or encryption will not affect screen readers when it actually blocks text access
Setting permissions in the PDF creation tool that inadvertently restrict accessibility
Not testing the secured PDF with a screen reader to verify that content is still accessible after applying security

examples.pass: A PDF has content copying restricted but the 'Content Copying for Accessibility' permission is explicitly enabled, allowing screen readers to access all text
examples.fail: A PDF has security settings that block both content copying and content extraction for accessibility, causing screen readers to encounter a completely blank document
examples.partial: The PDF allows text extraction for accessibility but the security settings trigger a warning in some screen readers, creating confusion even though access is ultimately permitted
